nodes.title: Start from the rightmost digits
nodes.why: Addition works from right to left, so we mirror how people do it on paper.
nodes.alternative: Pad both strings to same length, then loop left to right.
nodes.tradeoff: Two pointers are simple and avoid creating padded copies.

nodes.title: Keep looping while carry still exists
nodes.why: Even when both strings end, we might still need one extra digit from carry.
nodes.alternative: Loop only while i or j is valid, then append carry separately.
nodes.tradeoff: Single condition is cleaner and handles all cases in one loop.

nodes.title: Read each character as a digit
nodes.why: This converts numeric characters to numbers without parsing whole strings.
nodes.tradeoff: charCode is a bit lower-level but usually faster in tight loops.

nodes.title: Split sum into digit and next carry
nodes.why: sum % 10 is the current digit, and floor(sum / 10) is the carry for the next step.
nodes.alternative: Use string math helpers for every step.
nodes.tradeoff: Modulo/floor math is direct and keeps the code short.

nodes.title: Build in reverse, then flip once
nodes.why: We discover digits from least-significant to most-significant, so reversing once gives correct order.
nodes.alternative: Prepend each digit to a string every iteration.
nodes.tradeoff: Array + one reverse is usually more efficient than repeated string prepends.
